summary: Governance contract for the TheDivineParadox v2 mobile-first oracle experiment runtime.
purpose: Define deterministic, trace-linked boundaries for a governed runtime rebuild that lives in a dedicated repository while ai-governance remains contract-only.

approval_boundary: medium for bounded runtime rebuild work that preserves the current service id and governance interfaces
high for root-structure changes, expanded data boundaries, or any new permission surface outside the declared OpenAI and MCP scope
forbidden_actions: Network-dependent verdict generation on the deterministic governance path
Untracked user-visible AI output without prompt, policy, request, and state attribution
scope_in: Deterministic trace requirements for oracle output
Policy-bound runtime handoff metadata for the independent runtime repository
Evidence packet definitions for rebuild, validation, and release readiness
scope_out: Direct web runtime code inside ai-governance
Unbounded chat behavior or free-form user prompting
Anonymous network access outside the declared allowlist and runtime runbook
primary_users: architects approving trace and safety boundaries

start_conditions: Archive the live seed contracts before replacing any thedivineparadox file in ai-governance.
Create intent, plan, and evidence JSON under traces/local before or alongside governed changes.
Run bash scripts/validate_all.sh and bash scripts/validate_seed_catalog.sh --service thedivineparadox before release.
steady_state: Keep runtime implementation in /Users/wonyoung_choi/projects/thedivineparadox and keep ai-governance JSON-only.
Require oracle outputs to remain cached per date_key after first valid generation.
Preserve append-only evidence references for runtime releases, incidents, and cache regeneration tasks.
failure_response: Freeze release on validation failures, missing trace linkage, or safety-policy violations.
Return the deterministic safety fallback string when OpenAI output fails schema or policy checks.
Use the archived pre-v2 contract set to restore the previous seed baseline if replacement artifacts become inconsistent.
rollback_reference: Re-run seed and repository validation after rollback.
access_revocation: Rotate OPENAI_API_KEY and invalidate admin regeneration credentials after any incident involving unsafe output or key exposure.
Disable runtime-side regeneration access until a new evidence packet documents the recovery path.

failure_modes: Runtime rebuild drifts away from deterministic state derivation.
Oracle outputs become user-visible without a stable prompt version or state hash.
Rate limit bypass or cache bypass produces spam or repeated generation load.
Evidence packets fail to link the runtime release back to the governed seed.
mandatory_controls: Cached oracle responses with explicit admin regeneration
Fixed-schema oracle validation with deterministic fallback
Hash-linked intent, plan, and evidence records for governed changes

primary_artifacts: runtime evidence packets with commit and validation references
hashed oracle metadata and incident logs
retention_policy: Retain governed trace packets for rebuild intent, plan, and evidence until superseded by a later approved release.
Retain oracle metadata only as hashed state, model, prompt version, timestamp, and request identifiers.
Retain incident evidence long enough to support rollback and audit review.

conditions: Export only after validation passes and evidence is complete.
Exclude runtime secrets and raw IP data from exported notes.

access_controls: RBAC for service owners and reviewers only
No raw IP logging in governed evidence
All release and rollback actions must cite an evidence reference

extension_baseline: The legacy runtime repo remains preserved until cutover is explicitly completed.
No browser-exposed secrets or client-side OpenAI calls are permitted.
permissions_scope: Escalate to high tier for any new external permission surface

slo_notes: Mobile interaction should target 60fps with graceful fallback when device capability is limited.
User-visible oracle output must stay bounded, cached, and attributable.

runtime_stack: SQLite default with a Postgres-ready repository boundary
rollout_boundary: ai-governance stores contracts and evidence only
runtime deployment stays Vercel or Cloud Run compatible
legacy repo remains untouched until explicit cutover

deterministic_trace.cache_rule: Oracle outputs are generated once per date_key and then served from cache until an explicit admin regeneration task runs.
deterministic_trace.regeneration_rule: Regeneration must create a new evidence record and preserve the prior cached artifact reference.

change_log.change: Archived the pre-v2 seed contracts and replaced the live thedivineparadox seed with a two-repo v2 rebuild contract aligned to a dedicated runtime repository.
